reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: CSS: Cascading Style Sheets
sources.versionScope: Current CSS modules and evergreen browsers

claims.statement: CSS is a stylesheet language used to describe document presentation.
A CSS rule combines selectors with property-value declarations applied to matching elements.
Cascade and selector matching can prevent an apparently valid declaration from becoming the used style.

verifications.method: Change one selector property and confirm only the intended element changes visually.
verifications.expectedResult: Only the intended element changes visually.

verifications.method: Confirm the stylesheet is loaded and the selector matches the class, ID, or element in the HTML.
verifications.expectedResult: The stylesheet is loaded and the selector matches the class, ID, or element in the HTML.

verifications.method: Remove or override the rule and observe that the targeted presentation changes predictably.
verifications.expectedResult: That the targeted presentation changes predictably.

limitations: A selector match does not guarantee a declaration wins; cascade, specificity, inheritance, order, and validity can affect the result.
CSS affects presentation rather than changing the semantic meaning of the underlying HTML.
Individual CSS modules evolve separately, so version-sensitive features require their own compatibility check.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
